source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
article_title: How HMX took over the mid-tier linear slot
subject: Exploded-axonometric switch silhouette laid out as a four-tier vertical stack on a dark near-monochrome field. From top to bottom: top housing (parallelogram with cruciform stem aperture and a small LED notch), stem (POK cruciform head + body, the splash-cyan accent dot and outer ring anchor here at the cruciform head), spring (nine flat coil ovals with a faint dashed center axis), and lower housing (parallelogram with two pin contacts, center mount post, and PCB-mount nubs). Each tier is skewed +18px right at its top edge to give the silhouette a right-leaning axonometric tilt — the eye reads 'lifted apart and tipped toward the viewer.' Three faint dashed gap lines with small open dots mark the explode separations between tiers, naming the 'let's open this up' Deep-Dive metaphor. Four left-margin measurement ticks mirror the family rhythm. Single pale-cyan splash, single brass-bronze theme dot tucked at the upper-right margin as the family-convention anchor.
alt: Exploded-axonometric silhouette of a mechanical keyboard switch on a dark background — top housing, stem, spring, and lower housing separated by thin gap lines, with a single pale-cyan accent dot anchoring the stem.
prompt: Deep-Dives-pillar piece on HMX outflanking Cherry on MX2A's home turf via factory-lube competence, POK stem profile, deliberate $0.45-0.55 price tier, and 8-week restock cadence. Per bearings hero-art directive, Deep-Dives pieces lean cross-section / part-detail / acoustic-spectrogram visuals; the chosen subject is an exploded-axonometric switch silhouette so the visual literally states 'we are taking this apart to read its argument.' Pairs naturally with the article's load-bearing technical claims: the POK stem (anchored by the splash-cyan focal dot at the cruciform head) and the factory-lubed spring (the third tier). Splash hue is pale-cyan oklch(0.78 0.085 235) — chosen for two reasons. First, HMX is loosely associated in product photography with their POK light-blue / pale-cyan stems, and the switch is the HMX 'Cloud' which the hue echoes evocatively. Second, it slots cleanly into the pillar palette without collision: the steel-blue 225 tag-layout and 215 75-percent-default sit 10-20 hue-degrees away but are paired with rail/numeric subjects (not part-detail), the sage 145 / 155 family is 80-90 degrees off, the cherry-red 30 of cherry-mx2a-revision and the coral 30 of gateron-oil-king-deep-dive are 155+ degrees clear, and the warm-amber 60-75 of the bronze theme + typing-tests-lie's teal 195 are also clear. The closest neighbor is the recent-shipped warm amber, which is across the wheel. Cyan at 235 carries a faint 'instrument-grade / blueprint' connotation that suits a Deep-Dive teardown. Composition follows the family rhythm strictly: 1200x750 canvas, content centered around x=600 with ~83% inked width margin, full stack envelope y=150 (top of housing) to y=700 (bottom of lower housing) for ~73% height, four-tier vertical layout matching gateron-oil-king-deep-dive's tier rhythm but exploded-axonometric instead of cut-away. Each tier carries a parallelogram silhouette with +18px right-skew at its top edge — a single consistent axonometric tilt across all four parts. Family-canonical 2px warm-grey stroke for all silhouette outlines and detail lines, with stroke-opacity reductions (0.35 for explode-gap dashed lines, 0.4-0.6 for inner-cavity / well edges, 0.5 for skirt rails and mount nubs, 0.3 for didactic margin ticks). The single splash-cyan focal anchor at the cruciform head uses the family's filled-core (r=6.5, alpha 0.95) + outer ring (r=12, alpha 0.45) cursor convention, lifted from typing-tests-lie's peak cursor — keeps the eye on the stem, which is the article's central technical claim. The wash ellipse around the stem tier sits at 0.15 alpha (well below the splash band conventions of trends-tracker / typing-tests-lie at 0.07-0.14, but tighter to the focal piece since the wash is part-scoped not field-wide). Three explode-gap dashed lines (4 6 dasharray, 0.35 alpha) with small open center-dots literalize the 'let's open this up' metaphor. A single brass-bronze theme dot at (1148, 160) anchors the piece in the thock family palette without competing with the cyan focal at (600, 295) — diagonally opposite corner, family convention preserved.
